title: Chemical-free processes and methods of neutralisation, clarification, purification and decolourisation for sugar manufacturing and correlated effluents
abstract: The process, method and system for treating and purifying sugar-based mixtures involve providing a liquid form of a sugar-based mixture and filtering it through a liquid-solid separator using a filter aid bed with a specific mean pore size and density This process produces filter cake and sugar juice. The process engages neutralisation, clarification, purification and decolourisation in a single step process with zero retention time for treatment by mechanical means. The pH of the sugar- based mixture can be increased while passing through the filter aid bed. The method may also include steps of providing a liquid-liquid separator after the liquid-solid separator and crystallisation after the liquid-liquid separator. The sugar-based mixture can be selected from various sources, and the liquid form can be produced by employing various techniques.
claims: Claims 1. A process for treating sugar-based mixtures for production of white or refined sugar, comprising the steps of: providing a liquid form of a sugar-based mixture; filtering the liquid mixture through a liquid-solid separator using a filter aid bed, optionally comprising bagasse ash and/or fly ash, having a specific mean pore size between 6 to 30 micron and density between 160 to 300 kg/m 3 ; whereby filter cake and clarified sugar juice are produced; and, submitting clarified sugar juice to one or more evaporators. 2. The process of claim 1, wherein the process comprises the further step of filtering the clarified sugar juice through a liquid-liquid separator using one or a succession of membranes which are configured to increase the Brix of the sugar juice prior to being submitted to said one or more evaporators. 3. The process of claim 2, wherein the or each membrane increases the Brix of the sugar juice to reach a Brix of sugar juice of 19 to 65. 4. The process of claim 3, wherein the or each membrane is at least partially of ceramic material. 5. The process of any of the preceding claims, wherein the clarified sugar juice is submitted to a juice heater prior to being submitted to the or each evaporator. 6. The process of any one of claims 1 to 5, further comprising the step of neutralising the pH from 4 to 7 of the sugar-based mixture whilst passing through said filter aid bed. 7. The process of any one of the preceding claims, wherein said filter aid bed is configured to remove impurities from 0.1 to 0.5 microns whilst the liquid mixture passes through the filter aid bed. 8. The process of any one of the preceding claims, wherein said step of filtering said liquid mixture arises before any heating of said liquid mixture. 9. The process of claim 2, further comprising a step of crystallisation after said liquid-liquid separator. 10. The process of any one of the preceding claims, wherein said filter aid bed comprises a precoat filter layer disposed on a porous support means. 11. The process of any one of the preceding claims, wherein said filter aid bed comprises filter aids selected from one or a mixture of the following: siliceous volcanic rock, amorphous silicate, diatomaceous earth, expanded perlite, solkafloc, fly ash, bagasse ash, activated carbon, graphite, graphene, microcrystalline cellulose, cellulose fibre, bentonite, diatomite, oxidised graphite, oxidised graphene, agricultural fibres, saw dust, rich hull ash, paper fibres. 12. The process of claim 11, wherein one or more of said filter aids are combined with lime. 13. The process of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising the step of evaporation to obtain solid sugar crystals having sugar colour between 100 IU to 10 IU. 14. The process of claim 2, wherein the or each membrane comprises one or more of the following: silicon carbide, activated carbon, graphite, graphene, oxidised graphite, and oxidised graphene; optionally the pores range from 600 to 1200 Daltons. 15. The process of claim 2, wherein the or each membrane comprises sintered steel with cross-flow internal titanium pores ranging from 0.0001 to 0.02 microns. 16. The process of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the sugar-based mixture is selected from sugarcane juice, sugar beet juice, sweet sorghum juice, corn sugar syrup, sugar solutions, raw sugar melt, and solutions of said substances. 17. The process of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the liquid form is produced by employing one or more of the following or a combination thereof: rotary juice screen, mixed juice filter, melting, boiling, fluid stimulator, filtration, addition of milk of lime, dilution, oxidation, ozonation. 18. A system for treating sugar-based mixtures configured to carry out the process of any one of claims 1 to 17.
